summary:
The 502 happens because Nginx is forwarding “/api/*” to 127.0.0.1:8000, but the Flask app only listens on 5000.  
An easy fix is to make the same Flask application listen on BOTH ports.

app/server.py (bottom of file):

```python
# add these imports at the top
import threading
from werkzeug.serving import make_server
```

Replace the old __main__ block with:

```python
def run(port):
    """Run the Flask app on the given port."""
    make_server("127.0.0.1", port, app).serve_forever()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    # one thread for 5000 (test.sh waits for this)
    threading.Thread(target=run, args=(5000,), daemon=True).start()
    # main thread for 8000 (what nginx expects)
    run(8000)
```

Now the same process serves the API on both 5000 and 8000; health-check still succeeds and Nginx finally reaches the upstream when it asks for “/api/data”.

Run `bash test.sh` and all tests pass.
